plan_metadata.name: Feature Verify Wizard UI Loads All Project Types From Wizard Node

steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, selecting an agent type for Microsoft 365 Copilot.
@assertion "No Action", "Add an Action", "Add a Copilot connector" and "Start with TypeSpec for Microsoft 365 Copilot" exist.

Click the "Back" button (appearing as a left arrow with tooltip "Back (Ctrl+Alt+-)") at the top left of the quick action dropdown in the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog within Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents extension.

Click the "Custom Engine Agent" option in the "New Project" modal dropdown to start building an agent with your own orchestration and LLM, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion "Basic Custom Engine Agent" and "Weather Agent" exist.
Click the dropdown input field labeled "Select an option" at the top of the app template selection menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Click the "Copilot connectors" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, selecting the feature to embed organization data for use in Microsoft 365 Copilot.
@assertion "Add a connector name" exists.
Click the text input field labeled "Create a name for Copilot connector" in the Visual Studio Code workspace, to initiate naming a new Microsoft 365 Copilot connector.
Click the "Teams Agent and Apps" dropdown option in the New Project selection dialog within Visual Studio Code, under the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
@assertion "General Teams Agent", "Teams Agent with Data", "Teams Collaborator Agent" and "Other Teams Capabilities" exist.
Click the dropdown field labeled "Select an option" in the Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK modal within Visual Studio Code, to open agent type selection.
Click the "Office Add-in" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, as indicated by the red crosshair.
@assertion "Task pane", "Custom Function and Shortcut", "Create Declarative Agent with Office Add-in Action" and "Upgrade an Existing Office Add-in" exist.
Press the Esc key to close the "Select a capability" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Create a New Agent/App" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar within Visual Studio Code, as highlighted by the blue button at coordinates (349, 135).
Click the "Build a Declarative Agent" button in the left sidebar of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome screen within Visual Studio Code.
Use keyboard shortcut ctrl+shift+~ in Visual Studio Code to open a new integrated terminal while in the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" Welcome and guided tutorial interface.
Click on the "OUTPUT" link in the TERMINAL panel at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code interface within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Click the "Tasks" tab in the OUTPUT panel at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code window within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Type the letter 'm' into the "Filter" input field located in the OUTPUT panel of Visual Studio Code, used to filter output sources (e.g., Tasks, Extension Host, Pty Host) in the dropdown menu.

Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" option in the dropdown menu of the OUTPUT panel within Visual Studio Code, selecting the output channel for viewing logs or messages related to this extension.
@assertion "[Dynamic Template] Loaded wizardNode.json from bundled" exists.
